extraction_reasoning: The litigation table separates matters filed by and against each entity: I excluded all “By our Company” (3 criminal, 1 civil, ₹321.97 million), “By our Subsidiaries” (1 criminal, ₹6.88 million), and other “By” rows, and counted only against rows. Criminal proceedings against were 1 against the Company, 1 against Directors, 1 against Promoters, and nil against Subsidiaries, while statutory/regulatory proceedings against were nil; tax proceedings against were 22 against the Company, 9 against Directors/Promoters in their personal capacity (excluded from the tax field), and 39 against Subsidiaries, so the company-and-subsidiaries tax count is 61. The quantified aggregate amount against the Company and Subsidiaries is ₹251.92 million plus ₹1.10 million = ₹253.02 million, or ₹25.302 crore, excluding promoter/director personal matters and all “By” rows. The contingent-liabilities figure is the explicitly stated total of ₹1,734.89 million as at June 30, 2024, equivalent to ₹173.489 crore, rather than a re-sum of the listed items. Company-wide customer concentration for the latest full fiscal year, Fiscal 2024, is 40.13% for the top five and 56.77% for the top ten, measured against revenue from operations; all source monetary tables use ₹ million, converted to crore by dividing by 100.
